items.title: I'm Not Counting on Social Security COLAs to Carry Me Through Retirement. Here's What I'm Doing to Combat Inflation Instead.
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: The article discusses the author's plans to combat inflation in retirement, including investing in dividend-paying stocks, creating a detailed budget, and converting a traditional IRA to a Roth IRA.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article mentions The Coca-Cola Company as a dividend-paying stock that can provide above-average yields and dividend growth to combat inflation.
The article highlights Procter & Gamble as another dividend-paying stock with an above-average yield and a history of consistent dividend increases.
The article suggests that the author is no longer interested in owning growth stocks like Nvidia, as their recent strong performance may not be sustainable.
Similar to Nvidia, the article indicates that the author is not planning to own growth stocks like Amazon in retirement, as their recent outperformance may not continue.
